message:
🌱 Add keycloak: Consistent behaviour for User API getUsers and count mission (#993)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-38556-consistent-behaviour-for-user-api-getusers-and-count",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Consistent behaviour for User API getUsers and count",
+    "description": "The `/admin/realms/{realm}/users` and `/admin/realms/{realm}/users/count` endpoints have inconsistent parameter support. The count endpoint was missing filters like enabled, emailVerified, idpAlias, idpUserId, and exact that the users endpoint supports. This issue affects 22+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the inconsistent API behavior",
+        "description": "Verify the issue by comparing results from the User API endpoints:\n```bash\n# Check your Keycloak version:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config\n\n# Use kcadm.sh to query users vs count with the same filters:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users \\\n  -r myrealm --query enabled=false\n\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users/count \\\n  -r myrealm --query enabled=false\n```\nIf the count does not reflect the same filtering as the users list, you are affected by this inconsistency."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the root cause",
+        "description": "The `/users` endpoint supported filtering by `emailVerified`, `enabled`, `idpAlias`, `idpUserId`, and `exact`, but the `/users/count` endpoint did not accept or process these parameters. This caused the count to return totals that did not match the filtered user list."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for consistent User API behavior",
+        "description": "The fix adds support for the missing filter parameters to the `/users/count` endpoint:\n1. `/users` endpoint: Added support for filtering by `emailVerified`\n2. `/users/count` endpoint: Added support for `enabled`, `emailVerified`, `idpAlias`, `idpUserId`, and `exact` filters to align with the `/users` endpoint\n\nUpgrade to a Keycloak version that includes this fix. See the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/40265) for implementation details."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm consistent API behavior is restored",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by comparing filtered results:\n```bash\n# Query users with a filter:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users \\\n  -r myrealm --query enabled=false\n\n# Count should now match:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get users/count \\\n  -r myrealm --query enabled=false\n```\nConfirm that the count matches the number of users returned with the same filter parameters."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The `/users/count` endpoint was missing filter parameters that the `/users` endpoint supported. The fix adds `enabled`, `emailVerified`, `idpAlias`, `idpUserId`, and `exact` filter support to the count endpoint, ensuring both endpoints return consistent results when using the same query parameters.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",
+      "identity",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],

+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],

+      "pr": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/40265"
+    },

+  },

+    ],

+  },
